theme: Custom sorting network for pre-ordered wires
title: Pre-Ordered Wires: Sorting Networks That Skip Known Relations
innovation_hook: A sorting network only needs to resolve the incomparable pairs of the input poset; known relations are comparators you can delete.
strategies: insertion-sort network baseline: emit the full n(n-1)/2 compare-exchange bubble network on all wires, ignoring the partial order (reproduces the checker baseline, ~0.1)

general-purpose recipe: drop in Batcher's odd-even mergesort pruned to n wires -- fewer comparators than insertion sort, but it re-sorts inside the already-ordered runs and never reads the partial order

partial-order exploit: recover the already-sorted runs (chains) from the relations and only MERGE them (balanced-tree Batcher odd-even MERGE), deleting every comparator that would re-verify a known relation; spend the saved budget trimming depth via the alpha weight

beyond-strong headroom: minimal / optimal merging networks and size-depth co-optimized merge trees per the instance's alpha, plus best-known small sorting networks on the residual incomparable structure
